jokes.text: I don't see any blue on this menu.
jokes.setup: Someone tries to make a heartfelt toast
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately interrupts with bizarre food obsession
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank absurdity - completely derails a sincere moment with nonsensical food preference. Establishes his blue obsession that runs throughout the episode.

jokes.text: The best foods are blue.
jokes.punchline: Confident declaration of completely false nutritional wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Frank's delusional confidence about completely wrong information. Economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Now, Dennis, what will I be having this evening?
jokes.punchline: Frank treats Dennis like his personal food orderer
jokes.explanation: Shows the weird codependent dynamic between characters, setting up Dennis's frustration.

jokes.text: Oh, God. You people.
jokes.setup: Dennis tries to make heartfelt toast
jokes.punchline: Immediate exasperated dismissal of everyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissistic frustration when his moment is ruined. Great economy.

jokes.text: Ugh, this guy again.
jokes.punchline: Characters treat recurring server like an unwelcome pest
jokes.explanation: Sets up ongoing gag about their rudeness to service workers. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: We can't get away from you.
jokes.punchline: Treating normal person doing job like stalker
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing their self-centered worldview where everyone else is the problem.

jokes.text: If I wanted a compliment, I'd walk into a bar.
jokes.punchline: Frank misunderstands 'compliments' as verbal praise
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay misunderstanding that feels natural for Frank's character. Well-constructed.

jokes.text: You don't chew no good anymore. You want me to chew that up for you?
jokes.punchline: Charlie offers to pre-chew Frank's food like a baby bird
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting and specific to Charlie's weird caretaking instincts. Highly original and memorable.

jokes.text: He gets his teeth sharpened?
jokes.setup: Charlie mentions Frank's teeth sharpening routine
jokes.punchline: Dee's horrified realization this is a real thing
jokes.explanation: Good straight-person reaction to absurd information. Helps sell the weirdness.

jokes.text: I think he's choking.
jokes.setup: Frank is clearly choking to death
jokes.punchline: Casual, understated observation of life-threatening emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obvious crisis. Sets up their terrible response to emergency.

jokes.text: [Long pause while Frank chokes]
jokes.setup: Frank is choking and needs help
jokes.punchline: Everyone just watches in silence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of silence as comedy. The pause IS the joke - their complete inaction during emergency is perfectly crafted cringe.

jokes.text: You only ever talk about how you don't want to die, like in a hospital bed or getting spit-roasted by two Latin King gang members in prison.
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal fear to hyper-specific prison scenario
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from mundane to extremely specific and dark. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: You mash it too thin. You take all the flavor out of it. You get all the nutrients. I get all the mush.
jokes.setup: Frank complains about Charlie's food chewing
jokes.punchline: Detailed critique of pre-chewing technique like it's normal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that treats the disgusting arrangement as established relationship dynamic. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I used to do cool shit, Frank! I used to be cool!
jokes.punchline: Charlie Kelly claiming he used to be cool is inherently absurd
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's delusional self-image is always funny, though not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: getting spit-roasted by two Latin King gang members in prison
jokes.setup: Discussion of how Frank doesn't want to die
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific, graphic prison death scenario
jokes.explanation: Shock humor with Frank's criminal paranoia, but relies on graphic content

jokes.text: Why do I still have to order your dinners for you? And why is it up to me to decide whether or not you're going to save a man's life?
jokes.setup: Dennis complains about having to make decisions
jokes.punchline: Escalates from ordering food to life-or-death decisions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation that reveals how Mac literally can't function without Dennis. Perfect character dynamics.

jokes.text: If you told me to.
jokes.setup: Mac says he would have saved Dennis
jokes.punchline: Only if Dennis told him to, even for saving Dennis himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Mac's complete dependence on Dennis - he can't even save Dennis without Dennis's permission. Brilliant character comedy.

jokes.text: Pizza man, pizza man, I got pizza here, who wants pizza man?
jokes.punchline: Charlie's made-up delivery song
jokes.explanation: Typical Charlie randomness. Fits character but not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: What are you, like, in your 40s? Yeah. As are you.
jokes.setup: Charlie acts like midnight is early
jokes.punchline: Dennis points out they're the same age and both middle-aged
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment. Dennis's exasperation at everyone's delusions is solid character work.

jokes.text: I actually ate all the cheese on the walk over, so... no cheese!
jokes.setup: Charlie brings pizza for Dennis who can't eat cheese
jokes.punchline: Charlie ate all the cheese separately during delivery
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Charlie logic - trying to help but making it worse in the most bizarre way possible.

jokes.text: Don't worry about that, though. It's all good. I ate the crust, too.
jokes.punchline: Also ate the crust, making the situation even worse
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - just when you think it can't get worse, it does. Perfect Charlie logic.

jokes.text: Actually, I slurped that up, too, because that was just kind of looking good
jokes.setup: Charlie ate cheese and crust, leaving sauce
jokes.punchline: Also consumed the sauce, bringing nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect completion of the escalation. The image of Charlie slurping sauce is both disgusting and hilarious.

jokes.text: So you ate them all separately... all the elements... and then came over with an empty pizza box?
jokes.punchline: Mac's incredulous summary of the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that helps audience process the absurdity. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I got a pretty good rush from a man almost dying right in front of me. I-I liked it
jokes.setup: Dee reflects on Frank choking incident
jokes.punchline: Admits to getting sexual thrill from near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Sunny humor - character discovering disturbing truth about themselves. Very well-crafted psychological comedy.

jokes.text: You're so good at telling stories. So concise.
jokes.punchline: Dennis sarcastically compliments her terrible storytelling
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis sarcasm. Perfect cutting comment delivered with his characteristic smugness.

jokes.text: I wish it did hit you.
jokes.setup: Dee describes almost getting hit by car
jokes.punchline: Dennis casually wishes she had been killed
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual cruelty from Dennis. Economy and character integration are perfect - exactly what Dennis would say.

jokes.text: Go to bed? What are you, 40? We're the same age!
jokes.setup: Dennis says he's going to bed
jokes.punchline: Charlie repeats the age joke but Dennis immediately corrects him
jokes.explanation: Callback that reinforces the running gag about their age denial

jokes.text: [Awkward silence between Mac and Charlie after everyone leaves]
jokes.setup: Everyone else goes to bed, leaving Mac and Charlie alone
jokes.punchline: Neither wants to hang out with just the other
jokes.explanation: Great use of silence to show these characters don't actually like each other. The pause says everything.

jokes.text: I'm moving in.
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately moves in uninvited
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank boundary violation. Takes normal gratitude to psychotic extreme.

jokes.text: I could have been bashing your face in before you knew what hit you, which would've been this hammer, which I already had in my hand, in case you weren't listening.
jokes.punchline: Describes murdering the waiter with unnecessary detail
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank threatening violence while helping. The meta-commentary about listening is a nice touch.

jokes.text: That old vegetable lady is your mom?
jokes.punchline: Refers to disabled woman as 'old vegetable lady'
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank callousness. Dark but expected from his character. Not particularly original.

jokes.text: I threw a sheet over her head. She was freaking me out.
jokes.punchline: Put sheet over disabled woman like covering furniture
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly horrible Frank logic. Treats person like object in most casual way possible.

jokes.text: Are you insane?! We can't just play!
jokes.punchline: Dennis treats casual game like military operation
jokes.explanation: Good Dennis perfectionism/control freak behavior. Shows his inability to do anything casually.

jokes.text: Too hard. Too fast.
jokes.punchline: Dennis finds normal pass too intense
jokes.explanation: Shows Dennis's physical weakness and demanding nature. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Pollen?! You didn't tell me there was to be pollen today.
jokes.punchline: Dennis acts like Mac should control weather/seasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis entitlement - expects Mac to manage nature itself. Great character integration showing his impossible demands.

jokes.text: I went into the Puerto Rican part of town, started making fun of their big fat asses and asked them if they were Dominican.
jokes.punchline: Deliberately racist provocation for thrills
jokes.explanation: Dark Sunny humor showing Dee's callous thrill-seeking. Works for character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Take a break from your cockfighting and, uh, you know, hop in your rafts and come bring it to a real American
jokes.punchline: Mixes up racist stereotypes from different cultures
jokes.explanation: Shows Dee's ignorant racism but the joke is more about her stupidity. Somewhat lazy writing.

jokes.text: That's Mexican! / That's Mexican.
jokes.setup: Dee lists racist stereotypes about Puerto Ricans
jokes.punchline: Dennis and Mac point out she's confusing different nationalities
jokes.explanation: Good reaction showing Dee's ignorance is even worse than intended

jokes.text: You would put us all at risk with your suicidal race-baiting.
jokes.setup: Dee asking if they want to join her dangerous racist provocation
jokes.punchline: Dennis's clinical analysis of Dee's self-destructive behavior
jokes.explanation: Dennis's pseudo-intellectual analysis of obvious stupidity

jokes.text: I think she look nuts!
jokes.punchline: Waiter's horrified reaction to whatever Frank did
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to implied visual gag. Works but doesn't stand out particularly.

jokes.text: Made her dinner already. She's eating blue.
jokes.setup: Waiter explaining he fed Charlie's mother
jokes.punchline: References Frank's blue food obsession applied to invalid care
jokes.explanation: Connects earlier absurd food theory to current creepy caretaking

jokes.text: Somebody enrolled somebody back into pilot school.
jokes.punchline: Refers to himself in third person while manipulating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's manipulative generosity. Third person construction is funny touch.

jokes.text: Blue has the most antioxygens.
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'antioxidants' while being completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Good callback with malapropism. Frank's confident wrongness is always funny. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I want to throw this shake in your stupid face, but I'm struggling.
jokes.punchline: Too weak to perform simple angry gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect physical comedy showing Dennis's weakness. The struggle to do something so simple is brilliantly pathetic.

jokes.text: Dennis, do you want me to throw it into my own face?
jokes.setup: Dennis asks Charlie to throw shake in Mac's face
jokes.punchline: Mac offers to throw it in his own face to help Dennis
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac logic - so eager to please Dennis he'll hurt himself. Great character integration showing his desperation.

jokes.text: Are you feeding Dennis blended-up pizza and soda shakes?
jokes.punchline: Discovers Mac is poisoning Dennis with junk food disguised as health food
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment. The disgusting specificity of blended pizza and soda makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I want him to depend on me to take care of him, to bring him back from the brink of death.
jokes.punchline: Wants to create dependency through near-fatal poisoning
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Mac psychology - creates problems to solve them. Classic toxic relationship dynamic played for laughs.

jokes.text: Munchausen by proxy, that's cute and everything. But if you want Dennis to really depend on you, you're gonna have to save his life... for real.
jokes.punchline: Dee suggests immediate life-threatening poisoning instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and psychological insight. Dee casually names the mental disorder while suggesting something worse. Perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: She always said it's the last thing she ever wanted, so... So, thank you.
jokes.setup: Waiter thanks Frank for putting mother in home
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's grateful for doing the thing his mother most feared
jokes.explanation: Dark moment showing how Frank corrupts everyone. Waiter's relief about betraying his mother is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: I'm sticking her in a bang 'em and bin 'em joint.
jokes.setup: Frank put mother in nice home
jokes.punchline: Reveals he put her in terrible facility with crude name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Frank revelation. The crude terminology and casual cruelty is perfectly crafted. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: 15, 20 tops. Just until he flatlines. Then you bring him back.
jokes.punchline: Casually discusses letting Frank die for 15-20 minutes
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual discussion of murder. Dee's matter-of-fact delivery of horrific plan is brilliant dark comedy.

jokes.text: Because it's poisoned!
jokes.setup: Everyone arguing over who gets the shake
jokes.punchline: Mac blurts out the poison plot
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the poisoning plot. Mac's inability to keep the secret creates perfect chaos. Good structure.

jokes.text: I don't need you to tell me what to do, Dennis. I'm going to make my own decision.
jokes.setup: Dennis tells Mac to use the EpiPen on Dee
jokes.punchline: Mac chooses this life-or-death moment to assert independence
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant character moment. Mac finally asserts independence at the worst possible time. Perfect irony and character work.

jokes.text: I saw it! I saw the other side. I didn't like it. I hated it. It was just blackness.
jokes.setup: Dee wanted to see what's on the other side of death
jokes.punchline: Reports back that afterlife is disappointing darkness
jokes.explanation: Dark existential comedy. Dee's disappointed review of death/afterlife is uniquely twisted and memorable.
